field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: The critical conditions were established for hydroxyl-terminated PEG standards, ranging from Mw ≈ 200 to 2000 g mol⁻¹, using 1 mM LiCl concentration. The critical composition of the MeOH/water eluent was determined to be 88.8:11.2 (%, v/v) and this allows elution of all the PEG chains at the same retention time (tr ≈ 2.33 min) independently of their molecular mass.
field_evidence.critical_component: The critical conditions were established for hydroxyl-terminated PEG standards
field_evidence.column_name: The chromatographic column (octadecyl-silica Equisil, 150 x 4 mm, pore diameter: 100 Å, particle size: 5 μm)
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: The critical composition of the MeOH/water eluent was determined to be 88.8:11.2 (%, v/v)
field_evidence.mobile_phase_ratio: The critical composition of the MeOH/water eluent was determined to be 88.8:11.2 (%, v/v)
field_evidence.temperature_celsius: The chromatographic column was maintained at a constant temperature of 25°C.
field_evidence.pore_size: The chromatographic column (octadecyl-silica Equisil, 150 x 4 mm, pore diameter: 100 Å, particle size: 5 μm)
field_evidence.flow_rate: The flow rate was set to 1 mL min⁻¹.

conditions.critical_condition_basis: The retention of the SG1-capped PEO-b-PS copolymer was examined at the critical conditions of the PEO block in order to make the PEO segment chromatographically 'invisible'
conditions.physical_address: Universités Aix-Marseille I, II & III – CNRS, UMR 6264: Laboratoire Chimie Provence, Spectrométries Appliquées à la Chimie Structurale, F-13397 Marseille Cedex 20, France
conditions.notes: Critical conditions established using hydroxyl-terminated PEG standards ranging from Mw 200 to 2000 g mol⁻¹. Salt concentration varied between 0.1 and 1 mM but critical composition remained the same.
